subject: Physics
title: The Einstein-Bohr Debates
description: A 60-second lesson on The Einstein-Bohr Debates.

hook.text: Einstein and Niels Bohr argued about quantum mechanics for 30 years. Neither fully won.

buildup.text: Bohr said quantum mechanics was complete—randomness is fundamental. Einstein hated the idea.

discovery.text: Einstein kept inventing thought experiments to break quantum mechanics. Bohr kept finding the flaws.

twist.text: Einstein's 'failed' attacks actually deepened quantum theory. His challenges made it stronger.

climax.text: Modern physics sides with Bohr, but Einstein's skepticism drove breakthroughs like entanglement research.

punchline.text: Two giants argued for decades. Physics won.

quiz.question: What was Einstein's main objection to quantum mechanics?
options: That randomness shouldn't be fundamental to nature
That light can't be both a wave and particle
